description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves create a striped pattern of alternating light stone and deep black channels, giving the block a visually striking, high-contrast appearance. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a smooth, dark finish that contrasts with the textured top. The block appears robust and heavy, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The grooves on the top may provide additional grip or drainage, suggesting functionality beyond simple obstruction. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A heavy rectangular stone block with a grooved, striped top surface and smooth, dark sides, designed as an urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running from left to right across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the solid, dark side face of the block.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped top and the block's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the smooth, dark side surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
